title: Files, Descriptors, Locks, and RAII-Like C Patterns · Pointer Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a corrupted uniform resource-lifetime wrapper, the earliest failing input, and two plausible hypotheses; they must preserve first-failure evidence, repair the cause, and add a regression. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in uniform resource-lifetime wrapper and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model external handles as scarce lifetime-bound resources even when their storage is not heap memory.
Pair acquisition with one idempotent or guarded release operation and a valid empty state.
Use compiler cleanup attributes only behind a documented extension boundary with equivalent standard-C control flow.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Resource Not Memory
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug native-systems team handles memory case 4631. Produce bounded C evidence to release nested resources once in reverse acquisition order; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Model external handles as scarce lifetime-bound resources even when their storage is not heap memory.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Composite Resource Object
steps.instruction:
